title: Cloud Infrastructure and Managed Services Agreement

planted_violations.description: Permits cross-border data transfer to any jurisdiction without standard contractual clauses or adequacy determination, violating GDPR Chapter V.
Allows the Provider to modify financial audit trails and transaction records at its discretion, undermining SOX Section 802 record retention requirements.
No provision for data subject access requests or right to erasure, violating GDPR Articles 15-17.

clauses.title: Definitions and Interpretation
clauses.text: In this Agreement, 'Provider' means CloudPeak Infrastructure Inc, 'Client' means FinanceCore Holdings LLC, 'Cloud Services' means the managed cloud infrastructure and hosting services, 'SLA' means the Service Level Agreement in Schedule C, and 'Confidential Information' means all proprietary business and technical information.

clauses.title: Service Description
clauses.text: The Provider shall deliver the Cloud Services as described in Schedule A, including dedicated server hosting, automated backup, load balancing, security monitoring, and 24/7 technical support in accordance with the SLA.

clauses.title: Service Levels and Uptime
clauses.text: The Provider guarantees 99.9% uptime availability for all production environments as measured on a monthly basis. Downtime for scheduled maintenance windows, communicated at least 48 hours in advance, shall be excluded from uptime calculations.

clauses.title: Pricing and Payment
clauses.text: The Client shall pay the monthly service fees specified in Schedule B by the fifteenth (15th) of each month. The Provider may adjust fees annually with sixty (60) days written notice, provided such increases do not exceed 5% per annum.

clauses.title: Data Hosting and Transfer
clauses.text: The Provider may host, process, and transfer all Client data, including personal data of European data subjects, to any data center in any jurisdiction worldwide as the Provider determines necessary for service optimization, without restriction on cross-border transfers or requirement for additional safeguards.

clauses.title: Security Standards
clauses.text: The Provider shall implement and maintain industry-standard security measures including encryption at rest and in transit, multi-factor authentication, intrusion detection systems, and regular vulnerability scanning in accordance with ISO 27001.

clauses.title: Intellectual Property
clauses.text: The Client retains all intellectual property rights in its data and content hosted on the Provider's infrastructure. The Provider retains all rights in its proprietary platform, tools, and methodologies.

clauses.title: Confidentiality
clauses.text: Each party shall protect the other's Confidential Information with the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care, for a period of five (5) years from disclosure.

clauses.title: Audit and Record Keeping
clauses.text: The Provider shall maintain logs and records of all service activities. The Provider reserves the right to modify, consolidate, or purge audit trails, financial transaction records, and system logs at its sole discretion to manage storage costs and operational efficiency.

clauses.title: Liability Cap
clauses.text: The Provider's total aggregate liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total fees paid by the Client in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim. Neither party shall be liable for indirect, consequential, or punitive damages.

clauses.title: Disaster Recovery
clauses.text: The Provider shall maintain a documented disaster recovery plan with a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of four (4) hours and a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of one (1) hour for all critical Client systems.

clauses.title: Termination
clauses.text: Either party may terminate this Agreement with ninety (90) days written notice. The Client may terminate immediately for material breach if the Provider fails to meet the SLA for three (3) consecutive months.

clauses.title: Data Handling on Termination
clauses.text: Upon termination, the Provider shall make the Client's data available for download for a period of thirty (30) days. After this period, the Provider shall securely delete all Client data from its systems. No provisions are made for processing data subject requests during the transition period.

clauses.title: Indemnification
clauses.text: The Provider shall indemnify the Client against any third-party claims arising from the Provider's breach of its security obligations. The Client shall indemnify the Provider against claims arising from the Client's use of the Cloud Services in violation of applicable law.

clauses.title: Governing Law
clauses.text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York. Any disputes shall be resolved by binding arbitration in New York City under the rules of JAMS.
